acceptance_targets: [chapter_10_f01] Pass only if a cold reader can add the monthly outflows named in the revised TV-room briefing and arrive at Harry's stated post-Marcus total without inferring an omitted share, and if Rosa's hydroxychloroquine price uses one consistent unit in both her dialogue and Carmen's closing thought. Fail if any monthly amount or supply duration still requires guesswork to make the chapter's arithmetic work.

[chapter_10_f02] Pass only if the revised TV-room scene contains at least one concrete change in leverage or duty by the time it reaches snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:81 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:87: Carmen must either accept or refuse a specific operational role, expose a hole in the account that forces a new admission, or be tested by Agnes in a way that changes how the room treats her. Fail if the scene still reads as a mostly uninterrupted explanation session with Carmen as listener.

[chapter_10_f03] Pass only if the revised ending adds a new immediate consequence after Rosa leaves, such as a concrete choice in the lobby, at the desk, or in the medication room, and does not mainly restate the healthcare-arbitrage argument already made legible by snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:117, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:131, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:147, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:151. Fail if the final paragraphs still paraphrase the same moral and economic case in broader narration.

[PROSE_AUTOCOMM_CH10] PASS if: (a) the cited qualifier constructions are eliminated; (b) narrative passages between Agnes's dialogue turns are reduced by at least 30% in word count; (c) the chapter reads as a revelation scene driven by Agnes's dialogue and Carmen's reactions, not as narrated exposition with dialogue illustrations. FAIL if 'the specific/particular' or 'not X but Y' constructions remain, or if narrative bridges still re-explain Agnes's dialogue.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass only if a cold reader can add the monthly outflows named in the revised TV-room briefing and arrive at Harry's stated post-Marcus total without inferring an omitted share, and if Rosa's hydroxychloroquine price uses one consistent unit in both her dialogue and Carmen's closing thought. Fail if any monthly amount or supply duration still requires guesswork to make the chapter's arithmetic work.
findings.problem: The chapter promises a full financial architecture, but the numbers do not reconcile on the page. After Marcus's cut, Harry says 2800 dollars a month is distributed, yet the named destinations total only 2150 dollars if Agnes's envelope is the four-hundred-dollar share implied later. Rosa's cost math also slides between 'forty dollars' and 'forty dollars a month' for what the chapter later calls a ninety-day supply. Because chapter 10's central turn is Carmen grasping the ring as arithmetic, unreadable arithmetic undercuts the turn.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the money breakdown at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:19, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:27, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:31, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:33, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:35, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:37, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:43, then reconcile Rosa's pricing at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:109, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:113, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:117 with Carmen's recap at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:157 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:159. Either add the missing recipient or change the stated totals so the named outflows actually match Harry's monthly figure, and make Rosa's price unit consistent as total price versus monthly price versus ninety-day supply.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass only if the revised TV-room scene contains at least one concrete change in leverage or duty by the time it reaches snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:81 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:87: Carmen must either accept or refuse a specific operational role, expose a hole in the account that forces a new admission, or be tested by Agnes in a way that changes how the room treats her. Fail if the scene still reads as a mostly uninterrupted explanation session with Carmen as listener.
findings.problem: The opening scene delivers its revelation as a briefing, not a live dramatic contest. Harry and Agnes mostly explain while Carmen asks a few prompt-like questions and absorbs the case. That keeps the chapter informative, but not shortlist-level consequential: the reveal changes Carmen's thinking without forcing a concrete on-page risk, refusal, or operational choice in the room.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rebuild the explanatory blocks beginning at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:13 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:55 so at least one piece of information is won through resistance or a local test. Options: have Carmen challenge a hole in Harry's accounting, force Harry to answer why she should trust Agnes's books, or have Agnes make Carmen prove she can read a flagged case before the meeting continues. The target is not more exposition; it is a leverage shift that exists because this scene happened.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass only if the revised ending adds a new immediate consequence after Rosa leaves, such as a concrete choice in the lobby, at the desk, or in the medication room, and does not mainly restate the healthcare-arbitrage argument already made legible by snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:117, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:131, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:147, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:151. Fail if the final paragraphs still paraphrase the same moral and economic case in broader narration.
findings.problem: Rosa's body and her 'With what?' answer have already made the chapter's cost logic concrete, but the ending then translates the same point back into system language. The last beats re-prove what the scene has already landed instead of buying a new consequence.
findings.rewrite_direction: Compress or repurpose the parking-lot aftermath beginning at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:155, especially the recap at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:157 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:159. Once Rosa's bodily cost and Carmen's answerless exchange land at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:145 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:147, use the remaining space to show an immediate behavioral or operational consequence as Carmen starts shift rather than another abstract summary of surplus versus deficit.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if: (a) the cited qualifier constructions are eliminated; (b) narrative passages between Agnes's dialogue turns are reduced by at least 30% in word count; (c) the chapter reads as a revelation scene driven by Agnes's dialogue and Carmen's reactions, not as narrated exposition with dialogue illustrations. FAIL if 'the specific/particular' or 'not X but Y' constructions remain, or if narrative bridges still re-explain Agnes's dialogue.

evidence.excerpt: 1976: Agnes's share sat in a white envelope under her mattress. She didn't explain. Carmen looked at her — at the wire-rimmed glasses, at the precise hands still turning pill bottles label-forward, at the short gray hair and the slight leftward tilt from the stroke that had given her daughter the excuse to put her here. Agnes had no one to send the money to. No grandchildren. No church. No cause she believed in that needed four hundred dollars a month. Her daughter Helen visited every other Sunday with the children and talked to Agnes in the slow, loud voice of someone addressing a condition rather than a person, and Agnes sat through it with the compressed fury of a woman who could conjugate drug interactions in her sleep but who had been classified, filed, and stored. Her money accumulated under the mattress the way her expertise accumulated inside her silence — purposeless, precise, unclaimed.

2002: There was no bitterness in her voice. Agnes didn't traffic in bitterness. She trafficked in facts, and this was one: the institutional pharmacy that had substituted Burt's Parkinson's medication without reviewing his chart, that had delayed Agnes's own prescriptions for two weeks during a supplier switch three years ago while she sat in her room in pain that no one checked on because her chart said she was non-verbal — that pharmacy operated under the full authority of the state of New Jersey and the federal regulatory apparatus that licensed it. Agnes's composition notebooks, organized by diagnosis and screened for interactions by a pharmacist with four decades of retail experience, operated from a card table in a room that smelled like vinyl upholstery and the cold coffee Lola had smuggled from the kitchen. Carmen could see which system was more careful. She could see it in the handwriting.

2006: The numbers on the left Carmen recognized. A resident at Lakeview might be prescribed eight to twelve medications. The facility's pharmacy billed Medicare at institutional rates, and the per-resident monthly drug cost regularly exceeded two thousand dollars. Some of it necessary. Some of it the institutional reflex to medicate every symptom — agitation, insomnia, appetite loss, anxiety — that might otherwise require staff time the facility didn't have to give.

2064: "Flares." Rosa held up her right hand. The knuckles were swollen, the skin over them tight and shiny with the specific texture of chronic synovial inflammation — not bruised, not injured, just perpetually wrong, the joints thickened by a disease that treated her own tissue as hostile. "Mostly joints. My knees, my hands. Last time I went without — this was before Marcus — I ran out for three weeks. Couldn't open a jar. Couldn't button my pants." She looked at her hand the way you'd look at a tool you'd been issued that no longer functioned properly. "I called in sick for a week at a job I'd just started. A warehouse, data entry for their inventory system. They let me go after day four. The temp agency stopped calling after that."

findings.problem: Full-book pattern (all 18 chapters). This chapter is primarily expository — Carmen seeing the full operation for the first time. The auto-commentary pattern is particularly damaging here because exposition already risks didacticism; layering 'the specific/particular' qualifiers and 'not X but Y' corrections on top of informational prose creates double explanation. Specific instances: 'the specific' qualifiers near lines 1976 and 2064; 'the institutional reflex to medicate every symptom — agitation, insomnia, appetite loss, anxiety — that might otherwise require staff time the facility didn't have to give' (line 2006 — a catalogue explanation appended to an already-clear critique); Agnes's long explanatory speeches are in character, but the narration between them glosses what Agnes's dialogue already conveys. The chapter risks becoming a tour guide rather than a revelation.
findings.rewrite_direction: REPLACE — retain 0 instances. Target lines: (1) Lines 1976 and 2064: cut 'the specific/particular' qualifiers — let the nouns carry the weight. (2) Line 2006: compress or cut the appended catalogue ('agitation, insomnia, appetite loss, anxiety'). Carmen is a nurse; she doesn't need the narrator to list the symptoms she already knows. (3) Between Agnes's dialogue passages (lines 2000-2010 area), reduce narrative bridges that re-explain what Agnes just said. Agnes's speech is characteristically precise — the narrator doesn't need to translate it. (4) General: this chapter's revision should trust that the INFORMATION Agnes presents (notebooks, pricing, interactions) carries its own weight without the narrator qualifying and correcting the reader's understanding. Cut narrator bridges between dialogue turns by at least 40%.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Prioritize structural and craft blockers first: causality, chapter architecture, stakes clarity, continuity logic, and all HIGH/CRITICAL defects.
revision_pass.label: HIGH/CRITICAL structural/craft fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This is a dark comedy about elderly agency, institutional failure, and moral compromise in a setting most fiction treats as a punchline or a tragedy but never both. The risks the novel must take: render the comedy of octogenarians as pharmaceutical distributors without condescending to them or turning them into lovable rogues; make Carmen's escalating complicity fully legible without moralizing about it; show the bodily realities of aging — incontinence, tremor, cognitive decline, falls, the smell of a body that doesn't leave a building — with unsentimental specificity; render the economic desperation of both healthcare workers and uninsured buyers without turning either into social-issue abstractions; give the moral ambiguity of the ring genuine weight by refusing to resolve it into either 'they were wrong' or 'they were heroes.' The novel must trust readers to sit with complexity. When in doubt between too raw and too safe, choose raw. A nursing home that smells like potpourri is a nursing home written by someone who's never been in one.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism for bodily functions, aging, economic shame, institutional dysfunction, or the realities of nursing home care is a craft failure. The novel's comedy and humanity depend on rendering what polite fiction glosses over: what it smells like to clean someone who can't clean themselves, what it costs to count coins for a bus fare, what oxycodone sells for per milligram on the secondary market, what a concussion looks like on an 88-year-old skull, what it sounds like when someone with Parkinson's tries to hold a coffee cup steady, what Burt's dementia actually looks like up close. If the prose flinches from these specifics, the novel loses its authority.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the default for all dialogue. Uncontracted forms appear only for emphasis, character-specific formality (Agnes's low contraction level), Carmen under extreme stress reverting to formal speech, or deliberate register choices. In a contemporary New Jersey nursing home, stiff diction is the anomaly, not the norm.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate high-intensity scenes with reflective interiority. Vary scene count: single-scene chapters for maximum compression, three-scene chapters for range. Comic chapters (3, 9) should feel structurally different from quiet character studies (7, 8, 12, 15), which should feel different from crisis chapters (11, 16, 17). Follow a confrontation with a reflection, an escalation with a domestic scene, a comic set piece with an elegiac one. If two consecutive chapters share the same rhythm, one needs redesigning.
prose_style_profile.diction: Grounded and specific. Drug names (oxycodone, alprazolam, hydroxychloroquine), nursing procedures (med pass, pill count, charting, fall assessment), body realities (incontinence, tremor, laceration, scalp bleed). No purple prose, no literary performance. The style should feel like someone who works with their hands is telling you the truth about what she saw.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, varying by POV character. Carmen chapters are warm and sensory, anchored in the body and the immediate environment. Harry chapters are precise and calculating, with attention to numbers and angles. Agnes chapters are clinical and compressed. Lola chapters are winding and emotionally layered, with embedded memories. Earl chapters are spare and weighted, with silence doing as much work as language. Marcus chapters are kinetic, with physical movement and code-switching. Tammy chapters are flat and observational, her trained eye cataloguing what doesn't add up.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Varied by chapter and POV. Carmen chapters favor medium sentences with occasional long runs when she's anxious. Harry chapters are short and clipped. Comic set pieces (chapters 3 and 9) move between fast inventory and slow domestic beats. Quiet chapters (7, 12, 15) slow down and let space onto the page. No chapter should feel rhythmically identical to the one before it.
